title: Daily Financial Briefing
marketTone: CPI relief meets oil overhang — June inflation missed everywhere (core 2.6% y/y vs ~2.9% consensus), yields fell and futures turned up, but Brent >$86 and Warsh testimony keep hike anxiety alive.
summary: June CPI miss / downside surprise (8:30am ET): headline -0.4% m/m, 3.5% y/y (vs ~3.8% consensus, 4.2% May); core flat m/m, 2.6% y/y (vs ~2.9%) — largest monthly headline drop since April 2020; energy -5.7%, shelter +0.1% (BLS; consensus FactSet/CNBC).

Oil/geopolitics still the risk backdrop: WTI +9.4% to $78.14 and Brent +9.6% to $83.30 Monday; Brent pushed above $86 Tuesday after Trump reinstated an Iran blockade and floated a 20% Hormuz 'guardian' fee; US equities fell Monday (S&P -0.8%, Nasdaq -1.6%).
Asia split Tuesday: China A-shares V-recovery (Shanghai +1.36%, ChiNext +3.43%, >4,200 advancers); Nikkei -1.92%; Kospi +0.73% on foreign/institutional semi buying after Monday's sell-sidecar stress.
signal: Core disinflation arrived — but markets must decide whether a backward-looking June print outweighs forward-looking Hormuz oil risk and a hawkish Fed bench (Waller Monday, Warsh today).
watch: Warsh House testimony (10:00am ET) — does he treat the CPI miss as progress or dismiss it as pre-oil-spike noise? Treasury yield follow-through is the tell.
globalRegime: The Fed holds at 3.50%–3.75%, but the dominant question has shifted from 'how soon do hikes return?' to 'does June's core cool-down delay them?' June CPI delivered a broad miss vs consensus — headline 3.5% y/y (vs ~3.8%), core 2.6% y/y (vs ~2.9%) — yet Monday's oil spike and Governor Waller's Monday warning that several months of improvement are needed before holding steady mean the tightening narrative is dented, not dead. The July Monetary Policy Report still says inflation 'stepped up' this spring.
globalChanged: June CPI miss across the board: -0.4% m/m headline (vs -0.2% expected), 3.5% y/y (vs ~3.8%); core flat m/m (vs +0.2%), 2.6% y/y (vs ~2.9%). Services ex-energy flat; shelter +0.1%; energy -5.7% m/m but still +15.7% y/y (BLS; consensus FactSet/CNBC).
Post-CPI tape: Treasury yields fell sharply and equity futures turned mostly positive — initial relief, though traders note June data predates this week's oil spike (CNBC).
Geopolitical premium intact: Trump reinstated Iran shipping blockade + 20% Hormuz fee; Brent >$86 Tuesday; Kpler showed 14 Hormuz crossings Sunday vs 37 a week earlier (Reuters, CNBC).

Fed communication pipeline: Gov. Waller (Jul 13) said a hot core CPI would force near-term tightening consideration; Chair Warsh's prepared House remarks emphasize 'getting monetary policy right' ahead of 10am ET testimony (Federal Reserve, CNBC, PBS).
globalImplies: If yields stay lower after CPI and Warsh does not re-endorse near-term hikes, September hike odds could ease — but one month of core cooling is insufficient per Waller's own threshold.
Forward inflation risk from Hormuz/oil can overwrite June's gasoline-driven relief; July CPI will capture this week's energy spike.
Semiconductor stress persists into ASML (Jul 15) and TSMC (Jul 16) earnings despite Kospi's +0.7% Tuesday recovery on institutional buying.
globalTensions: CPI and oil are telling opposite stories — June headline fell on gasoline that has since surged ~10%. Markets rallied on disinflation data while Brent sits at one-month highs; whether equities hold gains through Warsh will reveal which narrative dominates.
chinaStance: Moderately loose via quantity, cautious on price — PBOC injected net CNY136.5bn Tuesday (CNY236.5bn 7-day repos at 1.40%) while holding rates; Q2 MPC reiterated 'moderately loose' policy and added 'enhance policy foresight, flexibility, and targeting' without signaling rate cuts amid imported energy inflation risk.
chinaChanged: A-shares V-recovery: Shanghai Composite +1.36% to 3,967.13, Shenzhen Component +2.77%, ChiNext +3.43%; >4,200 stocks rose; turnover ~CNY2.7tn (Sina Finance, Xinhua Finance).
Sector rotation: compute hardware/PCB and oil/gas led; semis materials and AI servers lagged — domestic positioning snapback diverging from US tech weakness (Sina).
PBOC Tuesday OMO: CNY236.5bn 7-day reverse repos at 1.40%, net +CNY136.5bn after CNY100bn maturity — continuing tax-period liquidity support (Yicai).
chinaImplies: Onshore rebound is tactical/sectoral, not a policy pivot — no LPR cut signal while global oil rises.
If US yields fall sustainably post-CPI, A-share hardware could extend gains; if Warsh re-hawkishness lifts yields, the bounce may fade.
Tax-period funding stress (Jul 15–17) likely means further PBOC injections this week.
chinaDivergences: A-shares bounced +1.4% to +3.4% while US Nasdaq fell 1.6% Monday and Nikkei lost 1.9% Tuesday — classic onshore vs offshore sentiment split. Kospi semis recovered (+0.7%) while China semis materials fell, suggesting uneven global chip sentiment.

signals.name: Core disinflation pivot — June CPI miss
signals.evidence: Core flat m/m, 2.6% y/y (vs ~2.9% expected); headline -0.4% m/m, 3.5% y/y (vs ~3.8%); shelter +0.1%; post-CPI yields fell sharply (BLS; consensus FactSet/CNBC).
signals.mechanism: Broad downside surprise vs consensus eases near-term re-hike urgency and supports duration/risk assets — unless Fed officials dismiss it as backward-looking.
signals.disprovedIf: Warsh explicitly rejects CPI progress and 2y yields reclaim 4.30%+ within 48 hours.

signals.name: Hormuz policy premium re-expanding
signals.evidence: WTI +9.4%, Brent +9.6% Monday; Brent >$86 Tuesday; Trump blockade + 20% fee; Hormuz crossings 14 vs 37 week earlier (Reuters, CNBC).
signals.mechanism: Supply-risk and shipping-cost shock raise forward inflation expectations, limiting how much CPI relief can reprice Fed path.
signals.disprovedIf: Brent falls below $78 and daily crossings exceed 40 without new escalation within a week.

signals.name: Fed tightening narrative dented, not dead
signals.evidence: CPI core 2.6% y/y miss vs ~2.9% consensus; but Waller Jul 13 said several months of improvement needed; Warsh prepared remarks stress price stability; oil >$86.
signals.mechanism: One soft core print lowers July-hike urgency but hawkish Fed bench + energy shock keeps September hike pricing alive.
signals.disprovedIf: CME FedWatch September hike probability falls below 30% and Warsh downplays hike risk in testimony.

signals.name: China onshore rebound is tactical
signals.evidence: Shanghai +1.36%, ChiNext +3.43% with hardware/oil leadership; PBOC net +CNY136.5bn without rate cut (Sina, Yicai).
signals.mechanism: Domestic positioning snapback and sector themes, not a monetary policy pivot.
signals.disprovedIf: Shanghai closes below Monday's intraday low on rising volume with broad northbound outflows.

signals.name: Semiconductor cycle still on trial
signals.evidence: Nasdaq -1.6% Monday; Kospi sell-sidecar Monday, +0.73% Tuesday on semi buying; ASML/TSMC earnings Jul 15–16.
signals.mechanism: Geopolitical risk-off plus peak-cycle fears hit high-duration AI/semi multiples ahead of capex guidance tests.
signals.disprovedIf: ASML bookings and TSMC capex guidance both beat and Nasdaq semis reclaim Monday's losses within two sessions.

signals.name: Backward-looking CPI may be a head-fake
signals.evidence: June energy -5.7% m/m on gasoline relief, but spot oil spiked >9% this week; CPI measurement period predates Hormuz escalation.
signals.mechanism: Markets may cheer the print then refocus on July energy pass-through.
signals.disprovedIf: Equities and bonds sustain a risk-on rally for two full sessions after CPI regardless of Warsh tone.

watchItems.headline: Warsh House testimony — CPI vs oil framing
watchItems.why: STRONG core-disinflation signal meets STRONG Hormuz premium; Warsh sets whether the CPI miss changes Fed reaction function.
watchItems.watch: House Financial Services hearing 10:00am ET; language on June CPI, energy shocks, 'policy firming,' and forward guidance.
watchItems.trigger: Warsh endorses near-term tightening or dismisses CPI as insufficient progress.
watchItems.invalidator: Warsh acknowledges disinflation progress and explicitly downplays September hike risk.

watchItems.headline: Post-CPI yield and equity follow-through
watchItems.why: STRONG core-disinflation signal; tests whether CPI relief sticks against oil overhang.
watchItems.watch: 2y and 10y Treasury yields vs pre-CPI ~4.62% 10y; S&P/Nasdaq cash session reaction after 9:30am ET.
watchItems.trigger: 10y yield falls below 4.55% and S&P reclaims 7,550 on volume.
watchItems.invalidator: 10y reclaims 4.65% and S&P fails to hold 7,500 by Wednesday close.

watchItems.headline: Hormuz fee/blockade implementation
watchItems.why: STRONG energy-premium signal; forward inflation risk that can overwrite June CPI.
watchItems.watch: Brent daily close; CENTCOM/US statements; Kpler/ship-tracking vessel counts.
watchItems.trigger: Brent holds above $85 for 3 sessions or crossings stay below 20/day.
watchItems.invalidator: Official walk-back of 20% fee and Brent below $80 with crossings >40/day.

watchItems.headline: ASML then TSMC earnings (Jul 15–16)
watchItems.watch: ASML sales/bookings vs €8.4–9.0bn Q2 guide; TSMC revenue/capex vs $39–40.2bn Q2 guide.
watchItems.trigger: Either company cuts H2/capex tone or misses bookings/guidance.
watchItems.invalidator: Both guide higher on AI demand and semis rally broadly.

watchItems.headline: Contrarian check — disinflation trade overdone
watchItems.why: If CPI and yields point dovish, crowded trade is long duration / short dollar.
watchItems.watch: Brent reclaiming $88 while 10y yields rise above 4.65% simultaneously.
watchItems.trigger: Both conditions met for 2 sessions = oil overwhelming CPI relief.
watchItems.invalidator: Core PPI (Jul 15) confirms disinflation trend with oil below $82.

sources: Bureau of Labor Statistics, Federal Reserve (Waller speech), CNBC, Reuters, Dimsum Daily, CNA, NST, TradingEconomics, PBS, Sina Finance, Xinhua Finance, Yicai, Yonhap Infomax, Asia Business Daily, Chosun Biz.
singleSource: Lipow ~$16/bbl Hormuz fee estimate (CNBC); Kpler 14 vs 37 Hormuz crossings (CNBC).
